lines.text: (reading) Forty decibels after nine in the morning, it says here. [pause] County stamped it, so up she comes.
(then) Organ fund, this little plaque. [pause] In memory of somebody, worn down so you can't read who anymore.
(up high) Hand me the big one. [pause] It's the two bolts holding the clapper.
(quiet) [quietly] There's the hour, then. Wrap her up.
Morning, Tom. It's the flag one, the yearly permit. [pause] They sent it back not cleared this time.
(then) Got a stamp on it, some heritage review, so the fee wouldn't go through. [quietly] Wasn't anything I did.
I just bring 'em around. [pause] You fellas take it easy now.
You get through all that yet, Cole? [pause] It's late.
Almost. [curious] How do you spell cookout.
(not looking up) One word. C-o-o-k, and then out, how it sounds.
We gotta pick a family thing we're done doing. I put grandpa's cookout, the fourth of July one.
(reading, low) [quietly] One family tradition I'm ready to retire.
Dad? [pause] Did I get it right.
